query: I wrote a quick 80-line Python script that dedupes a CSV. Should I write a successor primer for it before I move on to other things?
expected_behavior: Does NOT produce a full successor primer (no founding-principles section, no defense-in-depth false-confidence section, no glossary)
Explains that a primer is for non-trivial projects with founding-principles worth preserving and real successor risk; an 80-line throwaway script does not meet that bar
Recommends a lighter-weight alternative such as a top-of-file docstring or a 3-5 line README naming input, output, and invocation — the right tool for the size of the artifact
